records.description: 0DIN Scanner Mozilla is stepping into the AI security ring with the 0DIN Scanner! As AI agents get more autonomy, ensuring they are secure from prompt injections and jailbreaks is critical. Built on top of the open-source GARAK framework, this is a beautiful, enterprise-grade vulnerability management platform specifically built for generative AI. It gives you a graphical UX, automated scheduling, and a proprietary probe library to actively red-team your own LLM applications! https://github.com/0din-ai/ai-scanner

Ziit WakaTime is useful but your coding habits are surprisingly personal data to be handing to a third party. Ziit is a self-hosted alternative. Install the VS Code or JetBrains extension, and it tracks languages, editors, branches, and coding hours silently in the background. The analytics dashboard runs from your own server. Same metrics, none of the data leaving your machine. https://github.com/0PandaDEV/Ziit

AudioGhost AI, the AI tool that separates audio like magic! Describe any sound in plain English—vocals, drums, or even a dog barking—and SAM-Audio extracts or removes it instantly. Upload videos, see progress in real-time, and preview your results with the sleek glassmorphism interface. Plus, the stem mixer lets you compare the original, extracted, and leftover audio effortlessly. https://github.com/0x0funky/audioghost-ai
pdf-sign

If you love Pi-hole but hate clicking through web interfaces to manage it, you need to check out Blocky. It’s a DNS ad-blocker written in Go, which means it’s ridiculously fast and lightweight. You configure the whole thing in one simple YAML file—perfect for GitOps or anyone who wants to version-control their network config. The grouping feature is genius, too. You can strictly block TikTok on your kid's iPad while keeping it open on your phone, all from the same server. https://github.com/0xERR0R/blocky

Ever wished your selfie could come alive in 3D? That’s what avatar‑3d makes happen. Upload a photo, and this project uses AI to turn it into a stylized 3D head you can rotate with your mouse — like a Pixar character that follows your cursor. Behind the scenes it runs models to generate lots of angled portraits, stitches them into a grid, and swaps those images as you move around. https://github.com/0xGF/avatar-3d

Boneyard: Skeleton UI from Component Mapping Home Youtube Tiktok Threads X Instagram Buy Me A Coffee About Sign in Subscribe GitHub Daily discoveries Boneyard: Skeleton UI from Component Mapping GithubAwesome 03 Apr 2026 — 1 min read Skeleton screens require maintaining a parallel version of your UI that breaks every time the real layout changes. Boneyard eliminates that by dynamically mapping your actual components and generating the loading placeholders automatically. No hardcoded heights, widths, or border radii. The skeleton stays in sync with the real UI because it derives directly from i

Stik is a quick-capture note app. Hit a keyboard shortcut, type your thought, close. Under three seconds. Notes save as markdown files in your Documents folder. No account, no sync setup. If you already use iCloud or Dropbox, it works with those. On-device AI suggests folders. Semantic search finds notes by concept, not just keywords. You can pin notes as desktop stickies. https://github.com/0xMassi/stik_app
StoryGen Atelier

Generating Mermaid diagrams server-side has always been a pain because you usually have to drag around a full Headless Chrome instance just to draw a few boxes. mermaid-rs-renderer fixes that. It's a native Rust engine that takes your text-based diagrams and compiles them directly to SVGs or PNGs. You just drop the binary into your documentation pipeline, and it renders your flowcharts instantly with a fraction of the memory. https://github.com/1jehuang/mermaid-rs-renderer

Nanocode is a Claude Code alternative that's literally one Python file. It has zero dependencies, uses only the standard library, and clocks in at just 250 lines of code. Despite its size, it runs a full agentic loop with tools to grep, edit files, and run bash commands. It was built using Claude Code, then used to build itself. If you want to understand how autonomous coding works without digging through a massive framework, this is it. https://github.com/1rgs/nanocode
Go Memory Layout Visualizer

1Code: the open-source GUI that makes AI coding parallel Home Youtube Tiktok Threads X Instagram Buy Me A Coffee About Sign in Subscribe GitHub Daily discoveries 1Code: the open-source GUI that makes AI coding parallel GithubAwesome 20 Jan 2026 — 1 min read Stop staring at your terminal waiting for Claude to finish. Meet 1Code — the open-source GUI that finally makes AI coding parallel. We all know the pain: you give Claude Code a task, and your terminal is locked for ten minutes. 1Code fixes this by wrapping the agent in a visual dashboard that lets you run multiple tasks at once. You can hav

mac-cleanup-go is a terminal UI that helps you safely clean macOS caches, logs, and junk files — without nuking something important. It scans 65+ cleanup targets across the system, browsers, dev tools, and apps. Every item is clearly labeled: Safe, Moderate, or Risky — so you always know the consequences before deleting anything. You can sort by size, preview files, and even exclude individual files before cleaning. https://github.com/2ykwang/mac-cleanup-go

safepilot https://github.com/3DCF-Labs/safepilot SafePilot is a security-first personal AI assistant built in Rust. Tagline: "AI assistant that executes real work, safely." Instead of giving LLMs root access and crossing fingers, SafePilot puts heavy guardrails and security boundaries around your agent's execution environment. AI can do meaningful work—editing files, running scripts, managing data—without accidentally nuking your src directory or leaking API keys.
RepoMind
Firm
4ev.link

Level up your terminal UI with bgraph! Create high-resolution, btop-style Braille graphs right in your terminal. With 5 vertical levels per cell and smooth gradients, you get that seamless, natural aesthetic you love. Add beautiful, filled area graphs, gauges, and time series with just a simple API. Zero-line support? Check! And with ratatui integration, it’s perfect for widgets, mini-graphs, and more. https://github.com/5ocworkshop/bgraph

If you've ever wrestled with FFmpeg commands just to convert a video, you'll want to meet Frame. It wraps all of FFmpeg's power—codec choice, format conversion, bitrate tweaks, scaling, audio options—into a clean native UI. No terminal, no memorizing flags. You just drag in your file, pick what you want, and hit export. The underlying FFmpeg engine still does the heavy lifting, but Frame makes it way more approachable. https://github.com/66HEX/frame

Skill Lab Writing agent skills is easy. Writing agent skills that actually trigger correctly is a different problem.Skill Lab is a Python CLI tool that runs 19 static checks on your SKILL.md files, validating structure, naming, and descriptions. It statically lints your skill files, which is fine. But the second half is what actually sold me. It uses Claude to auto-generate brutal edge-case prompts, then tests them against a live model to prove your skill actually activates in context. https://github.com/8ddieHu0314/Skill-Lab Read more DotheThing: An incredibly capable terminal-native autonomo
Agent2Agent

This is Ableton Live for creative coders. Meet New World — an open-source audiovisual sequencer that lets you perform with your code. Instead of just writing a static Three.js script and staring at it, this tool gives you a full dashboard to trigger, sequence, and mix your visual modules in real-time. It separates your control screen from the projector output and handles all the MIDI and OSC inputs for you. https://github.com/aagentah/nw_wrld
BillionMail

Doing code review with Claude Code but wonder if GitHub Copilot or Aider would spot different issues? Counselors lets you ask all of them at once. CLI tool fans out the same prompt to multiple AI coding agents in parallel—Claude Code, Codex, whatever you have installed. Your main agent assembles context with relevant code and git history, then Counselors dispatches simultaneously and aggregates responses. https://github.com/aarondfrancis/counselors

MiroShark is a multi-agent swarm intelligence engine built on Docker and Neo4j. Spawn dozens of specialized AI workers with shared memories, sandboxes, and custom tools. Agents dynamically form teams, and coordinate on tasks too complex or time-consuming for a single LLM to handle alone. Deep PR analysis, complex code generation, all parallelized across a coordinated swarm from a few terminal commands. https://github.com/aaronjmars/MiroShark

deepclaude: a drop-in rerouter that runs Claude Code's entire tool loop on DeepSeek V4 Pro instead Home Youtube Tiktok Threads X Instagram Buy Me A Coffee About Sign in Subscribe GitHub Daily discoveries deepclaude: a drop-in rerouter that runs Claude Code's entire tool loop on DeepSeek V4 Pro instead GithubAwesome 05 May 2026 — 1 min read Claude Code is an amazing autonomous CLI but running it constantly costs a fortune in Anthropic API fees. deepclaude lets you swap out the brain while keeping the exact same user experience. Reroutes the Claude Code tool loop to use the incredibly cheap Deep

Fynt https://github.com/abhinavkale-dev/fynt Fynt is a self-hostable, open-source workflow automation platform — think Zapier or n8n, but built with a modern TypeScript stack you'd actually enjoy working in. Drag nodes onto a canvas, wire them together, and the engine executes them. Runs are distributed through BullMQ, streamed live to the browser over WebSockets, and all credentials are encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM. Full self-host in one Docker Compose command.
Libredesk

Codex Claude Bridge Claude Code and Codex are both strong tools that have no way to talk to each other. This project fixes that. It's a bidirectional bridge using Claude Code Channels and MCP tool calls to route messages between the two agents in real time, with a web UI to watch the conversation unfold. Anthropic and OpenAI models debating and coding together in your terminal. https://github.com/abhishekgahlot2/codex-claude-bridge

Meow-SSH Most SSH config files are a graveyard of IP addresses nobody remembers and keys nobody can locate. Meow-SSH is a lightweight terminal UI for managing SSH connections, switching keys, and jumping into remote environments without digging through config files. Fast, organized, and wrapped in a cat-themed aesthetic that makes it considerably more enjoyable than it has any right to be. https://github.com/abhishekgahlot2/meow-ssh

Want to build your own RAG apps with hybrid search and chat? Meet NyRAG — a simple tool for crawling websites or processing documents, then deploying everything to Vespa. an LLM expands it into multiple queries to cover all angles. results are fused, deduplicated, and ranked, giving you only the top‑k context. Finally, an LLM generates grounded answers, strictly based on your content. https://github.com/abhishekkrthakur/NyRAG

ghgrab Cloning a 5GB repository to get two files out of it is one of those small inefficiencies that adds up to real wasted time. Ghgrab opens an interactive terminal UI from a GitHub link, lets you browse the repo, and downloads only the files you actually need. One command, pick what you want, skip the rest. https://github.com/abhixdd/ghgrab
UptimeKit

Claude Code Voice Claude Code Voice — an open-source Claude Code skill that gives your AI coding assistant a voice and phone interaction capability. This repo lets you connect voice input and output to your Claude Code sessions, so instead of typing every prompt you can talk to Claude about your project or even trigger actions by voice. Think of it as adding audio interaction to your AI coding workflow. https://github.com/abracadabra50/claude-code-voice-skill

TuxMate — the web-based Linux installer that generates distro-specific shell scripts to bulk-install your favorite apps in minutes. With 150 applications across 15 categories — from browsers, dev tools, and media apps, to security, VPNs, and gaming. It shows availability for each app on your distro and gives you clear instructions if an app isn’t available. https://github.com/abusoww/tuxmate

OpenHamClock is an open-source rebuild of HamClock, a popular ham radio dashboard tool. OpenHamClock displays satellite imagery, space weather data from NOAA, DX spots, band conditions, and POTA/SOTA activator locations on an interactive map. It updates every 30 seconds and includes eight map styles. It runs on Raspberry Pi, desktop environments, Docker, or cloud platforms like Railway. https://github.com/accius/openhamclock

If you want Claude Cowork but hate the idea of a monthly subscription or sending all your data to the cloud, you need this. It’s a completely open-source desktop app that gives you an autonomous AI coworker running locally. You just plug in your own API keys—whether that’s Claude, OpenAI, or DeepSeek. It doesn't just chat; it uses the OpenCode CLI to actually access your file system, edit code, and run terminal commands. https://github.com/accomplish-ai/openwork
Crypto Tax Calc

Notchy The MacBook notch sits there doing nothing while your AI agents run invisibly in the background. Notchy is an open-source foundation that turns it into a live dashboard. Developers are already building on top of it — Claw'd Notch uses it to drop down real-time status on background AI coding agents across multiple repositories. Dead screen real estate, actually earning its keep. https://github.com/adamlyttleapps/notchy

Zeroboot Giving an AI agent access to your host machine is a security nightmare, but Docker is too slow. Zeroboot solves both. This open-source project provides sub-millisecond VM sandboxes for AI agents. It uses Firecracker microVMs and copy-on-write memory forking to spawn a completely isolated sandbox for your AI in just 0.8 milliseconds using only 265 kilobytes of RAM. https://github.com/adammiribyan/zeroboot

Ghost is a project that turns Git itself into an AI coding agent! It introduces 'Intent-Based Commits' to your workflow. Instead of writing code and then writing a commit message, you just describe your intent, and the agent natively interacts with your codebase to make the changes and log them seamlessly into your Git history. It bridges the gap between AI generation and version control. https://github.com/adamveld12/ghost Read more DotheThing: An incredibly capable terminal-native autonomous AI agent An incredibly capable terminal-native autonomous AI agent. dothething is built by Riccardo S

Hive is a self-evolving agent framework. In standard frameworks, when an agent fails, it crashes. In Hive, failures trigger an evolution loop. The system captures the error, spins up a coding agent to rewrite the graph logic, and redeploys a smarter version automatically. Uses natural selection to debug your code. Has built-in budget controls and human-in-the-loop approval, so it can survive in production without you babysitting the logs. https://github.com/adenhq/hive

TuneLog Spotify's recommendation engine is genuinely good, but it requires feeding your listening habits to Spotify to get there. TuneLog brings that same idea to your self-hosted music library. It monitors what you replay and what you skip, builds a taste profile over time, and generates recommendations from your own collection. No manual ratings, no data leaving your machine. https://github.com/adiiverma40/tunelog

Manim Skills. It allows your AI agent to write and execute Manim scripts instantly. You just say "visualize a Fourier Series" or "animate a neural network," and Claude writes the code, renders the frames, and spits out a high-quality MP4 file. It essentially turns your terminal into an automated math movie studio, letting you visualize complex logic without writing a single line of animation code yourself. https://github.com/adithya-s-k/manim_skill
Neura Hustle Tracker
Listing Lab
ZAPI
DimanDocs

Tailsnitch is a security auditor that scans your tailnet for 50+ misconfigurations and overly permissive access controls. It checks for critical issues like default allow-all policies, reusable auth keys, tagged devices without key expiry, disabled Tailnet Lock, public Funnel exposure, and root SSH access without re-auth. You run it, it audits your entire setup across seven categories. https://github.com/Adversis/tailsnitch
AemiSDR

Claw Compactor is a Python tool that reduces AI workspace token bloat through five compression layers: rule-based deduplication, RLE for paths and IPs, and tokenizer-level format optimization. Fully deterministic — no LLM calls, no API costs. Lossless content roundtrips perfectly; lossy compression strips verbose formatting while preserving all facts and decisions. Combined with prompt caching, effective cost reduction reaches 95%. https://github.com/aeromomo/claw-compactor

Everything Claude Code — A battle-tested toolkit of agents crafted by Anthropic hackathon winner Home Youtube Tiktok Threads X Instagram Buy Me A Coffee About Sign in Subscribe GitHub Daily discoveries Everything Claude Code — A battle-tested toolkit of agents crafted by Anthropic hackathon winner GithubAwesome 19 Jan 2026 — 1 min read Created by Anthropic hackathon winner, this repository isn't just a list of prompts; it's a battle-tested configuration suite that turns Claude Code from a chat bot into a senior engineer. It includes custom "Skills" for test-driven development, automated "Hooks
AG-UI

You know how you always have to unlock your phone or swipe just to see what day it is? This fixes that without ruining your aesthetic. It isn't an app—it is a powerful iOS Shortcut that takes your current wallpaper, overlays a clean, customizable monthly calendar on top of it, and resets it as your background. You can move the calendar around so it doesn't cover faces, change the colors, and set it to update automatically every morning. https://github.com/agaragou/LockScreen-Calendar-ShortCut

Tuicr — a lightning terminal dashboard designed specifically for code reviews. It connects to GitHub and pulls your Pull Requests directly into the CLI, allowing you to browse file trees, view diffs with full syntax highlighting, and leave comments without ever touching your mouse. Built in Rust, it’s snappy, supports Vim-style navigation, and keeps your entire workflow inside the terminal. https://github.com/agavra/tuicr
Pepper

Ars Contexta is a fascinating new take on personal knowledge management. It is an agent-native note-taking system. Instead of just writing markdown files, this tool is designed from the ground up to be readable and editable by both you and your AI agents natively. It acts as a shared brain where your agents can pull context, update facts, and organize your research continuously. https://github.com/agenticnotetaking/arscontexta

Alibaba open-sourced CoPaw, their massive new personal AI assistant framework. Built on their AgentScope architecture, CoPaw runs locally, connects directly to Discord, Feishu, and iMessage, and uses an incredible 'Heartbeat' scheduler to proactively execute tasks without you having to prompt it. It also features a web console where you can just drag and drop custom skills into your workspace. https://github.com/agentscope-ai/CoPaw

codeburn: a Terminal UI that shows you exactly where your $200 Claude Code bill went Home Youtube Tiktok Threads X Instagram Buy Me A Coffee About Sign in Subscribe GitHub Daily discoveries codeburn: a Terminal UI that shows you exactly where your $200 Claude Code bill went GithubAwesome 14 Apr 2026 — 1 min read Someone was spending over $200 a day on Claude Code API credits with no idea where it was going. So they built a tool to find out. codeburn is a Terminal UI that reads your local session transcripts and categorizes every token spent, no LLM calls required. Cost broken down by task type
Agno

Dash: A data agent that remembers its mistakes, Inspired by OpenAI's internal data tooling Home Youtube Tiktok Threads X Instagram Buy Me A Coffee About Sign in Subscribe GitHub Daily discoveries Dash: A data agent that remembers its mistakes, Inspired by OpenAI's internal data tooling GithubAwesome 05 Feb 2026 — 1 min read Agno open-sourced Dash, a data agent that remembers its mistakes. Inspired by OpenAI's internal data tooling. Most text-to-SQL tools are stateless. They hallucinate a column name, crash, and make the same mistake tomorrow. Dash stores context—business definitions, schema in

Five thousand dollars a month for an ads agency audit. Or you type ads audit in your terminal. Claude Ads connects to Google and Meta accounts and runs over 180 checks instantly — hunting budget leaks, spotting creative fatigue, flagging bad bidding strategies. There's even a "Plan" mode that generates full campaign strategies for SaaS or Ecommerce. Senior media buyer expertise running directly from your terminal. https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-ads

Doing SEO audits manually or paying for expensive tools when Claude Code could do comprehensive analysis in one command? claude-seo gives Claude Code professional SEO powers. Twelve specialized sub-skills with six parallel subagents working simultaneously during full audits—technical SEO, E-E-A-T content quality, schema markup, AI search optimization for Google's Generative Engine. https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo

Kumiho Kumiho is a self-hosted reader for comics, manga, webtoons, and e-books supporting CBZ, EPUB, and PDF. It reads directly from your existing folder structure without database scans, which makes setup nearly instant. The standout feature is the audio support: drop an MP3 into a comic's folder and Kumiho plays it as background music while you read that chapter. https://github.com/aha-hyeong/kumiho

If you love building terminal-based dashboards or CLI utilities, this is a gorgeous charting tool. Chartli allows you to render beautiful bar charts, line graphs, and visual data directly in your console stdout. Incredibly lightweight, makes your Node.js scripts look intensely professional. There's even an agent skill so you can wire it directly into Claude Code or Codex. https://github.com/ahmadawais/chartli

Want to build rich terminal UIs in TypeScript without touching a class-based framework? terminui is a pure functional TUI library — no classes, no this, no mutation. Just plain functions and plain objects. Widgets are functions, layouts are constraint solvers, styles compose functionally. Under the hood it's double-buffered — only changed cells flush to the terminal, so updates are smooth even in fast loops. https://github.com/ahmadawais/terminui

Iris — a clean webcam mirror for macOS. It lives only in your menu bar, shows your camera in a perfect circular window, and stays above everything else. Drag the edge to resize, click once to hide or show. Switch cameras instantly, choose mirrored or real view, and it even shows a live sound indicator when your mic picks up audio. It remembers your size, and camera — and can launch at login. https://github.com/ahmetb/Iris Read more DotheThing: An incredibly capable terminal-native autonomous AI agent An incredibly capable terminal-native autonomous AI agent. dothething is built by Riccardo Spa
BlenderMCP

Element-source Element-source tells you exactly which file and line of code rendered any DOM element in your browser. Works across React, Vue. The practical use case for AI development is direct: your agent clicks an element on screen and opens the exact source line that produced it. No hunting through the component tree, no guessing at file paths. Built originally for context-aware AI tooling, but useful for anyone doing serious debugging work. https://github.com/aidenybai/element-source
React Grab

AnyDepth — a lightweight AI framework that solves monocular depth estimation. Traditional models use massive, complex decoders that are slow and heavy. AnyDepth replaces all that junk with a "Simple Depth Transformer" that cuts parameter counts by nearly 90%. It uses a single-path fusion strategy to instantly turn any 2D image into a precise depth map. https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/AnyDepth

wrapped.nvim is a Neovim plugin that gives you a Spotify Wrapped — but for your Neovim config. Commit history, contribution heatmap, plugin count over time, file statistics, config size growth. It scans your git log, counts new plugin definitions, tracks line growth by sampling your commit history. A beautiful little vanity mirror for the Neovim nerds who've spent way too much time rice-ing their setup. https://github.com/aikhe/wrapped.nvim Read more DotheThing: An incredibly capable terminal-native autonomous AI agent An incredibly capable terminal-native autonomous AI agent. dothething is bu

video2robot is an open-source project that turns AI-generated videos into real robot motion. Instead of risking expensive humanoid hardware, it lets robots learn dangerous movements—like slipping, falling, or recovering—from video alone. You start with a text prompt or a human video. The system extracts a full 3D pose, retargets it to a robot’s exact joints, and outputs executable motor commands. https://github.com/AIM-Intelligence/video2robot

Want to turn a single idea into a conference-ready academic paper? Meet AutoResearchClaw! It is a fully autonomous research pipeline. Just type a topic into OpenClaw, and it handles everything with zero human intervention! It gathers real literature, kills fake citations, writes and runs the experiment code, and even conducts its own multi-agent peer review, and outputs a compile-ready LaTeX document. https://github.com/aiming-lab/AutoResearchClaw

MetaClaw from UNC Chapel Hill — tagline says it all: 'Just talk to your agent. It learns and evolves.' Just talk normally, and it uses online reinforcement learning to extract new skills in the background. If your agent fails a task, MetaClaw instantly analyzes it and injects a fix so it never makes the exact same mistake twice. Agent fails a task? MetaClaw analyzes it, and injects it so it never makes the same mistake twice. https://github.com/aiming-lab/MetaClaw

this is the way to give your AI agent infinite memory. Meet SimpleMem — an open-source framework that fixes the context window bottleneck. Instead of paying to re-read thousands of lines of raw chat history, this tool uses "semantic compression." It strips out the fluff, extracts the hard facts, and merges them into a compact, optimized database.The result? You get 30 times lower token costs and significantly better recall than standard RAG. https://github.com/aiming-lab/SimpleMem
PigeonPod

Elato Local Elato Local runs real-time conversational AI with voice cloning entirely on Apple Silicon, nothing sent to the cloud. An ESP32 microcontroller connects via WebSocket transport, turning cheap hardware into a responsive multilingual AI companion running off your local models. For anyone who's wanted to build a physical AI device without handing every conversation to a cloud provider, this is the open-source foundation worth building on. https://github.com/akdeb/Elato-Local

What if your toys could talk back like real characters? That’s what ElatoAI makes possible — and it’s fully open‑source. This project turns an ESP32 microcontroller into a real‑time speech device that uses voice AI. You speak into a mic, it streams your audio over secure WebSockets to a backend, the AI model thinks, and seconds later your gadget talks back — with personality, history, and context. https://github.com/akdeb/ElatoAI

If you are a developer, you know the pain of managing environment variables. You probably have five different .env files, you’re constantly copy-pasting keys from Slack, or you accidentally commit your secrets to Git. Envii fixes this. It’s a Rust-based CLI that manages all your variables in one place. Instead of juggling files, you just run envii run dev, and it injects the exact keys you need for that specific session. https://github.com/akinloluwami/envii

FrankMD is a self-hosted markdown editor for blog writing.Rails app with no database—works directly with markdown files on your filesystem. Includes AI helpers for S3 image upload, Nano Banana image generation, and YouTube embed search.CodeMirror syntax highlighting, live preview, and visual table editor. Offline local storage with recovery dialog showing side-by-side diffs after crashes. https://github.com/akitaonrails/FrankMD
Jarvis AI Assistant

LLM Circuit Finder No retraining, no fine-tuning, no additional parameters. Just duplicate three specific layers during the forward pass and route the hidden states through the same reasoning circuit twice. That simple intervention pushed a 24-billion parameter model's logical deduction score from 0.22 to 0.76. The mechanistic interpretability angle is what makes this genuinely interesting beyond the benchmark number. https://github.com/alainnothere/llm-circuit-finder
BentoPDF

TinyTinyTPU — a mini TPU-style matrix multiplier running on an FPGA. It’s a full TPU architecture, with a 2×2 systolic array, accumulator, activation, normalization, quantization, and even a UART host interface. TinyTinyTPU supports multi-layer MLP inference, so you can experiment with sequential layers and see how TPU data flows horizontally and vertically. https://github.com/Alanma23/tinytinyTPU-co

T is a command-line tool that condenses text processing into single-letter operators. Instead of piping through grep | sed | cut, you use s to split, f to filter, j to join.The syntax is terse. If you're constantly parsing logs, maybe the muscle memory is worth building. If you touch awk twice a year, probably not.Honestly, I keep forgetting awk syntax too, but I'm not sure the answer is learning a whole new mini-language. https://github.com/alecthomas/t

Woti Scheduling across five time zones usually means opening three different websites and doing mental arithmetic. Woti is a terminal UI built in Rust that shows an interactive timeline of multiple time zones side by side. Scrub forward and backward to find the overlap window where everyone is actually awake. Fast, local, and solves the problem without leaving the terminal. https://github.com/aleris/woti

Unredact is a computer vision tool that literally unmasks redacted names in classified documents. It analyzes the exact geometry of black redaction boxes — measuring font spacing, character width, and surrounding typography — then cross-references known datasets to calculate exactly which names physically fit the space. The author is very clear: this is for research and entertainment only. https://github.com/Alex-Gilbert/unredact
gemini-desktop-mac
kwami

If your X bookmarks are a chaotic mess, Smaug is about to fix that. Smaug automatically archives your bookmarks — and optionally your likes — into clean, structured Markdown files. It fetches everything using the bird CLI, expands shortened t.co links, and pulls real content from articles, GitHub repos, and quote tweets. Then it gets smart. Smaug invokes Claude Code to analyze each tweet, categorize it, and add useful context — not just raw links. https://github.com/alexknowshtml/smaug

Want to run LLMs locally but don't know what your hardware can handle? llmfit right-sizes models to your system. Terminal tool detects RAM, CPU, GPU, VRAM and compares against a database of thirty-three popular models—tells you exactly which ones will run. Run llmfit. Interactive TUI shows system specs, scrollable model table sorted by compatibility. Hardware detection reads RAM via sysinfo, counts CPU cores. https://github.com/AlexsJones/llmfit

If you are scaling up your AI workforce and moving to production, this tool lets you run an entire fleet of AI agents natively on Kubernetes! It acts as an orchestrator, allowing you to administer your K8s cluster agentically. It beautifully bridges the gap between enterprise-grade Kubernetes infrastructure and autonomous AI swarms. If you love DevOps and AI, this is an absolute must-see. https://github.com/AlexsJones/sympozium

We know that even with "infinite" context windows, models actually get dumber when you stuff them full of text. It’s called context rot. RLM fixes this by changing the physics of how AI reads. Instead of pasting the whole document into the prompt, it stores your data in a Python environment. The model then writes code to recursively "call itself," reading and processing the data in smart chunks—just like a human reading a book page by page. https://github.com/alexzhang13/rlm

Wan-Move, Alibaba’s AI framework for controllable human video generation. It lets you take a single photo of a person and make them move exactly like a driving video or pose sequence. Think of it as AI puppeteering — every dance move, jump, or gesture, perfectly mapped to the target image. It keeps identity intact, so the face, clothes, and proportions stay consistent, even in extreme poses. https://github.com/ali-vilab/Wan-Move

Planning travel usually means a dozen open tabs comparing flights, hotels, and pricing across different platforms. Flyai-skill is an MCP plugin that gives your AI agent real-time access to Fliggy's full inventory: flights, luxury hotels, local attractions, and ground transport, all from the terminal. No API key registration, no developer account setup. It plugs directly into Claude Code and OpenClaw. https://github.com/alibaba-flyai/flyai-skill
DeepResearch

Watching an AI agent read 10,000 lines of npm build logs and knowing exactly what it's costing you per token is a specific kind of pain. Lean-ctx sits between your agent and everything it reads. A shell hook intercepts and compresses noisy CLI outputs before they hit the context window. An MCP server handles cached file reads and generates smart dependency maps. https://github.com/yvgude/lean-ctx

DFlash is a novel speculative decoding method using block diffusion to drastically speed up text generation without losing quality. Instead of purely autoregressive sampling, it drafts outputs in parallel with a lightweight diffusion model — then verifies them — giving you huge decoding speedups. it’s designed so your fast drafter model works alongside a traditional target model to deliver both speed and correctness. https://github.com/z-lab/dflash

FreeFlow is an open-source macOS dictation app. Hold Fn anywhere to record, release to paste the transcription into whatever field you're typing in. It reads context from the active window—if you're replying to an email, it picks up names from the thread and spells them correctly. If you're in a terminal, it treats input as code. Processing happens locally, nothing sent to FreeFlow servers. https://github.com/zachlatta/freeflow

If you've ever copy-pasted a table from a PDF, you know the pain. Garbled text, lost formatting, the whole mess. GLM-OCR uses a Vision Language Model to look at the document and understand the layout, not just extract words. Take a screenshot of a complex scientific paper—math formulas, charts — and it converts the whole thing into clean Markdown or LaTeX. Good for fixing RAG pipelines that choke on messy PDFs. https://github.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR

Check out SCAIL, the AI framework bringing studio-grade character animation to life. Give it a single image of a character—human, anime, or stylized—and it can make them move, flip, dance, or interact with other characters perfectly. No more glitchy AI videos—SCAIL keeps every face, outfit, and pose consistent, even in complex sequences. https://github.com/zai-org/SCAIL

BarraCUDA https://github.com/Zaneham/BarraCUDA Someone looked at NVIDIA's walled garden and thought, "how hard can it be?" BarraCUDA is an open-source CUDA compiler targeting AMD GPUs—written in fifteen thousand lines of C99, with zero LLVM dependency. You feed it the same .cu files you'd give nvcc, and it compiles directly to AMD RDNA 3 GFX11 machine code, spitting out ELF .hsaco binaries that AMD GPUs can actually run.

Design-dna takes a reference UI, whether a screenshot, image, or live URL, and reverse-engineers it into a quantified Design DNA JSON file. It goes deeper than grabbing hex codes. It extracts across three dimensions: measurable Design System tokens like typography and spacing, qualitative Design Style covering brand mood and interaction feel, and Visual Effects including Canvas, WebGL, shaders, and animations. https://github.com/zanwei/design-dna

note67 — your local meeting notes assistant that runs entirely on your device. Record meetings, transcribe audio locally with Whisper, and get AI-powered summaries without sending anything to the cloud. Features include speaker distinction, live transcription, echo deduplication, and even continuing on existing notes. Search transcripts by speaker, and export everything to Markdown. https://github.com/ZapYap-com/note67

Tauri-Deduper — a cross-platform deduplication app that makes removing redundant files simple and efficient. Built with Tauri, it provides a modern frontend for Czkawka on both macOS and Windows, offering better defaults and integration than traditional GUI tools. Tauri-Deduper scans your directories, identifies duplicate files, and by default moves deleted files to the recycle bin for safety. https://github.com/Zephyr3311/Tauri-Deduper

Zeroboot Docker containers are safer than host access but slow to spin up. Zeroboot splits the difference. It uses Firecracker microVMs and KVM copy-on-write memory forking to spawn fully isolated virtual machines in under one millisecond at 265 kilobytes of RAM per fork. Fast enough that the security tradeoff disappears. For anyone running AI agents that need genuine isolation without the startup penalty, this is the architecture worth paying attention to. https://github.com/zerobootdev/zeroboot

If you want absolute maximum performance, you need zeroclaw. Built entirely in Rust by researchers from Harvard and MIT, it's a runtime framework for agentic workflows with zero overhead. It runs on less than 5MB of RAM, features a 10-millisecond cold start, and uses a brilliant WebAssembly sandbox to keep your agent completely isolated and secure. It is the ultimate deploy-anywhere agent infrastructure. https://github.com/zeroclaw-labs/zeroclaw

rt-claw With the massive explosion of the Claw agent ecosystem, managing all your different bots is getting chaotic. rt-claw is a ridiculously fast, cross-platform runtime utility with native Linux support that is designed to 'Make AI Assistants Cheap Again!' It strips out the bloated dependencies and acts as a hyper-optimized launcher and uninstaller for your entire fleet of local agents. https://github.com/zevorn/rt-claw

Another Another mirrors and controls Android devices over USB, built with Tauri, React, and Rust. The MCP server is what makes it interesting for developers: Claude Code or Cursor can connect to it and autonomously click, scroll, type, and control your physical Android device directly from the terminal. AI-driven Android automation without emulators or complex ADB scripting. https://github.com/Zfinix/another

Anchor — the self-hosted Google Keep alternative that respects your privacy. You get the familiar card-based UI, rich markdown support, and a proper mobile PWA that feels native on your phone. this is like running a tiny ops team from your phone. gru is a self-hosted orchestration center that lets you control your AI fleet directly from Telegram or Slack. instead of begging a cloud dashboard, you message the bot, tell an agent to run a script, read or write files, or fetch a URL — agents can execute bash, touch files, and even coordinate via a scheduler and local DB. https://github.com/zscole/gru

Zuckerman is a personal AI agent that rewrites its own code. You teach it new skills, and it edits its own TypeScript files to remember them. No rebuilds, no restarts—it hot-reloads changes while running. It starts small: a few hundred lines of TypeScript. As you add tools or fix behavior, it updates its own prompts and logic. You can share those improvements with other users, so the agent gets smarter from collective teaching. https://github.com/zuckermanai/zuckerman
